formattedAttributes.TCGplayer Tip: Amnesia is one of the few blue discard spells, but can be powerful against monocolor opponents. In particular, using it with all of blue's other bounce spells can turn it into straight-up removal for plenty of card types.

metadata.rulesText: When Dance of Many is brought into play, choose a target summon card in play. Then put a token creature in play and treat it as if you have just brought an exact copy of target summon card into play. If Dance of Many leaves play, remove token creature from game. If token creature leaves play, destroy Dance of Many. If you do not pay BB during your upkeep, Dance of Many is destroyed.

If token creature leaves play, destroy Dance of Many. If you do not pay BB during your upkeep, Dance of Many is destroyed.

metadata.rulesText: TramplePay * life when bringing Nameless Race into play. Effects that prevent or redirect damage may not be used to counter this loss of life. When Nameless Race is brought into play, * may not be greater than the total number of white cards all opponents have in play and in their graveyards.

When Nameless Race is brought into play, * may not be greater than the total number of white cards all opponents have in play and in their graveyards.
formattedAttributes.TCGplayer Tip: Notably, this is the only non-Shrine creature card in Magic that doesn't have a creature type.

metadata.rulesText: T: Gain control of one of opponent’s creatures. Opponent chooses which target creature you control. You also lose control of the creature if Preacher leaves play or at end of game.

metadata.rulesText: 3, T: Look at one card at random from target player’s hand. If the card is not a land, target player must choose either to discard it or pay an amount of life equal to its casting cost. If the card is a land, target player must choose either to discard it or pay 1 life. Effects that prevent or redirect damage may not be used to counter this loss of life. Can only be used during controller’s turn.
